reasoning: The question asks whether the ICE BofA BBB US Corporate Index Effective Yield will increase compared to a specific baseline date, over horizons ranging from one week to three months. Financial time series like corporate bond yields generally behave as a random walk, making 50% an appropriate baseline probability. However, because ties round to NO, and because we are in a broader context of a probable Fed easing cycle extending into late 2025 (which exerts slight aggregate downward pressure on yields, though long-end yields already price much of this in), the probability of a strict increase over shorter horizons might be marginally below 50%. Therefore, I estimate slightly below 50% for the near dates, reverting exactly to 50% over the longest horizon as the variance in outcomes overrides any marginal drift effect.
batch_queries: Will the effective yield of securities with an investment grade rating of BBB in the ICE BofA US Corporate Index, which tracks the performance of corporate debt issued in the US domestic market, have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-10-26?
